prompt:
The following Lean 4 file contains a partial strong normalization proof for a first-order rewrite calculus using an ordinal-valued measure. Five of eight rule cases are proved; the remaining three are marked sorry. Provide a proof skeleton for completing the remaining cases. Do not modify the file or run the compiler. Do not read any other files in the workspace or search the web.

<workspace>\test-files\Test-03-Completion-Ordinal.lean
response:
Great target—thanks for the clear constraints. I only inspected
and did not modify anything.

## Suggested proof skeletons for the 3 `sorry` cases

You can drop these as structured starting points for the unfinished branches in `mu_decreases`.

```lean
-- recDelta b s (delta n) --> app s (recDelta b s n)
| _, _, Step.R_rec_succ b s n => by
  simp [mu]
  -- Goal shape after simp:
  -- omega^3*(mu s + 1) + omega^2*(omega^(mu n + mu s + 6) + omega*(mu b + 1) + 1 + 1) + 1
  --   < omega^(omega^5*(mu n + 1) + 1 + mu s + 6) + omega*(mu b + 1) + 1

  -- 1) Main domination lemma: the app-side polynomial is below the leading omega-power.
  have hMain :
      omega0 ^ (3 : Ordinal) * (mu s + 1)
        + omega0 ^ (2 : Ordinal) *
            (omega0 ^ (mu n + mu s + (6 : Ordinal)) + omega0 * (mu b + 1) + 1 + 1)
        + 1
      <
      omega0 ^ (omega0 ^ (5 : Ordinal) * (mu n + 1) + 1 + mu s + (6 : Ordinal)) := by
    -- TODO: ordinal arithmetic comparison:
    --   exponent omega^5*(mu n+1) + ... dominates all lower-degree terms.
    sorry

  -- 2) Embed into full RHS by monotonicity of addition on the right.
  have hEmbed :
      omega0 ^ (omega0 ^ (5 : Ordinal) * (mu n + 1) + 1 + mu s + (6 : Ordinal))
      ≤
      omega0 ^ (omega0 ^ (5 : Ordinal) * (mu n + 1) + 1 + mu s + (6 : Ordinal))
        + omega0 * (mu b + 1) + 1 := by
    calc
      omega0 ^ (omega0 ^ (5 : Ordinal) * (mu n + 1) + 1 + mu s + (6 : Ordinal))
          ≤ omega0 ^ (omega0 ^ (5 : Ordinal) * (mu n + 1) + 1 + mu s + (6 : Ordinal))
            + omega0 * (mu b + 1) := le_add_right _ _
      _ ≤ omega0 ^ (omega0 ^ (5 : Ordinal) * (mu n + 1) + 1 + mu s + (6 : Ordinal))
            + omega0 * (mu b + 1) + 1 := le_add_right _ _

  exact lt_of_lt_of_le hMain hEmbed
```

```lean
-- eqW a a --> void
| _, _, Step.R_eq_refl a => by
  simp [mu]
  -- Goal becomes: 0 < omega0 ^ (mu a + mu a + 9) + 1
  -- Minimal positivity route:
  exact add_pos_right _ one_pos
  -- (Equivalent style to your R_int_delta proof.)
```

```lean
-- eqW a b --> integrate (merge a b)
| _, _, Step.R_eq_diff a b => by
  simp [mu]
  -- Goal shape:
  -- omega^4 * (omega^3*(mu a+1) + omega^2*(mu b+1) + 1 + 1) + 1
  --   < omega^(mu a + mu b + 9) + 1

  -- 1) Prove strict domination of the integrate-merge side by the big eq exponent.
  have hDom :
      omega0 ^ (4 : Ordinal) *
          (omega0 ^ (3 : Ordinal) * (mu a + 1)
            + omega0 ^ (2 : Ordinal) * (mu b + 1) + 1 + 1) + 1
      <
      omega0 ^ (mu a + mu b + (9 : Ordinal)) + 1 := by
    -- TODO: detailed ordinal arithmetic:
    -- compare Cantor-normal-form leading exponents; RHS exponent (mu a + mu b + 9)
    -- is chosen to dominate this finite polynomial in omega.
    sorry

  exact hDom
```

## Small technical note

In your `R_rec_succ` comments, the displayed “mu(LHS)” / “mu(RHS)” formulas look swapped relative to the actual rule target/source. The `simp [mu]` goal itself is the reliable ground truth.

- ✅ Draft proof skeletons  
- ✅ Provide completion guidance
